note: World-side action templates per gap × ailment-category. Order = most-likely-to-work first. 38.3 recommendRemedy fills {initiativeId}, {category}, {neighborhood} placeholders from pattern data.

default.rationale: no existing mitigator addresses this ailment
default.expectedEngineEffect: {category} metrics stabilize if council passes + agent implements

default.action: mayor's office highlights the gap in public remarks
default.rationale: surfaces the issue into council agenda
default.expectedEngineEffect: approval / visibility shift feeds civic-load metric

default.rationale: initiative is the right remedy but has not moved — unblock the responsible agent or scheduled action

default.action: mayor's office directs civic-project agent to expedite review
council votes on expediting milestone review
default.rationale: adds political weight behind the advance

default.action: layer a second {category} initiative alongside the existing one
default.rationale: one mitigator is firing but ailment severity has not dropped — insufficient magnitude

default.action: neighborhood organizer / local leader drives ground-level action

default.rationale: mitigator is firing and severity is dropping
default.expectedEngineEffect: severity drops to medium/low next cycle

techSideTriggers.note: Tech-side fallback fires only when the writeback chain itself is broken. Detected when: initiative status=passed AND implementationPhase indicates rollout/active AND effectsFiring=false AND priorAuditCount>=3 (enough cycles to rule out noise).

bugReportHints.transit: Check phase02/applyInitiativeImplementationEffects.js — transit effects on Sentiment + Transit_Metrics
bugReportHints.economic: Check phase06/economicRippleEngine.js + phase10 persistence for RetailVitality
bugReportHints.sports: Check phase05/runAsUniversePipeline.js + phase08 writers for EventAttractiveness
